subject: Cloud Computing
title: Data Warehouses in the Cloud
description: A micro-lesson that makes Data Warehouses in the Cloud usable.

hook.text: Your company generates millions of events per day. Where do you store and analyze them? A data warehouse.

buildup.text: Data warehouses — Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake — are optimized for analytical queries across massive datasets.

discovery.text: They use columnar storage: instead of reading entire rows, they scan only the columns you need. Much faster for queries.

twist.text: Data warehouses are terrible for real-time transactions. They're built for asking questions, not processing orders.

climax.text: Snowflake became a $70 billion company by making cloud data warehousing simpler than anyone else.

punchline.text: Data warehouses answer big questions. Not handle small transactions.

quiz.question: Why do data warehouses use columnar storage?
options: It scans only needed columns instead of entire rows, making analytics faster
Columnar storage is cheaper than row-based storage
